articles.title: Relief from heatwave likely from Monday, says BMD - Newagebd.net
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Chhattisgarh
articles.full_text:
Bangladesh Meteorological Department on Sunday forecasted that the mild to moderate heatwave, sweeping over Khulna division and the districts of Tangail, Gopalganj, Rajshahi, Pabna, Patuakhali would abate from Monday, following rain and thunder showers over the country till April 10.
The BMD considers 36 – 37.9 degree Celsius as mild and 38 – 39.9 degree Celsius as moderate heatwave.
Meteorologist Tariful Newaz Kabir told New Age on Sunday that rain or thunder showers accompanied by temporary gusty wind and lightning flashes would likely occur at one or two places over Rangpur, Rajshahi, Khulna, Barishal, Chattogram and Sylhet divisions with hails at isolated places on Monday.
The forecast for Tuesday said that the same weather would occur at a few places over Khulna, Barishal, Chattogram and Sylhet divisions and at one or two places over Rangpur, Rajshahi, Dhaka and Mymensingh divisions.
‘The temperatures will increase after April 10,’ Tarique said.
On Monday, weather may remain mainly dry with temporary partly cloudy sky elsewhere over the country, according to the BMD weather bulletin.
On Saturday, the highest temperature 38.5 degrees Celsius was recorded in Chuadanga.
